all_citations.ref: DLR FY25 10-K (fiscal year ending 2025-12-31) Item 7 MD&A and Item 8 financial statements
DLR FY25 10-K Item 1 Business: 310 data centers, 30+ countries, 57.6M rentable sq ft (9.7M under development, 4.7M held for development), 84.7% leased
DLR FY25 10-K Item 1 Business and Item 7 MD&A customer concentration: 5,000+ customers; no single customer represented more than approximately 11.7% of aggregate revenue
DLR FY25 10-K Item 8 financial statements: FY25 total revenue $6.11B (+10% YoY); FY25 Core FFO/share $7.39 (+10.1% YoY); FY25 dividend per share $4.88

DLR FY25 10-K Item 7A: Net debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA 4.9x; total debt outstanding $18.4B ($17.5B unsecured + $0.9B secured); fixed charge coverage 4.5x; debt-plus-preferred-to-total enterprise value 26.1%

DLR Q1 2026 earnings 8-K press release 2026-04-23: Q1 2026 Core FFO/share $2.04 (+15% YoY); total bookings $707M at 100% share / $423M at DLR share; backlog $1.8B; record 0-1MW + interconnection bookings $98M (+40% YoY); 200MW Charlotte AI lease signed with AA-rated hyperscaler
DLR Q1 2026 earnings 8-K: full-year 2026 Core FFO/share guidance raised by $0.10 to $8.10 midpoint (implies +9% YoY growth)
DLR Q4 2025 earnings 8-K 2026-02-05: Q4 2025 Core FFO/share $1.86 (+7.5% YoY); Q4 revenue $1.63B (+13.8% YoY); FY25 same-capital cash NOI growth 7.7%
DLR 2026 proxy statement DEF 14A (executive compensation + beneficial ownership)
13F top holders Q1 2026 (Vanguard, BlackRock, State Street, Cohen & Steers, T. Rowe Price)

DLR Q1 2026 earnings call 2026-04-23 (CEO Andy Power, CFO Matt Mercier): seven consecutive quarters with largest signing from different hyperscaler; record interconnection bookings; 19-month average lease commencement lag; 21% of signings AI-oriented; over 1 GW of additional land capacity secured in Q1
